title: Public / External Review Export Safety Pack
decision_rationale: GL-218 documents the safety boundaries for a possible future public/external review export without creating the export. It answers all six required questions (eligible files, excluded files, allowed/prohibited claims, required checks, required approvals, remaining blockers). A local-only dry-run safety scan helper is added. No production, real-data, compliance, official-SDK, or live-PostgreSQL-production claims are made. All hard rules and prior safety boundaries from GL-207 through GL-217 are preserved.

current_public_external_review_posture.posture: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries

eligible_public_external_review_material: SECURITY.md (reviewed, routes to GitHub Security Advisories)
docs/openapi.yaml (API contract only, no real credentials)
website/index.html (static baseline only, no production hosting implied)
Reviewed backend source files (no real secrets, subject to per-file scan)
Reviewed test files using only synthetic/demo data
Reviewed docs/ artifacts for resolved issues (not internal-infrastructure references)

secrets_and_credentials: Raw PostgreSQL DSNs with real hostnames or passwords
Raw tokens, auth headers, or Bearer tokens
PEM private key blocks (RSA, OPENSSH, EC, DSA)
Real admin tokens or operator tokens
Real SMTP, S3, or cloud credentials
real_data: Real customer data or grant applications
Real institutional data or personal data (PII)
Real private grant data or private attachments
Database dumps, backup files, or logs with real data
internal_infrastructure: Private operator usernames or workspace IDs
Internal CI/CD config referencing private services
forbidden_files_and_paths: Any docs/website_design_* or docs/website-design-* import/report files
Package publishing config or registry metadata
Analytics/tracking integrations or data collection forms
Snapshot publish scripts unless separately reviewed
Production deployment config (Kubernetes, Terraform, Helm, cloud)
Release metadata, release tags, public release branches
data/ directory with real operational data

allowed_public_claims: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries
Local evaluation and controlled pilot only
Tenant/workspace isolation: baseline implemented, not production-complete
Ephemeral live PostgreSQL validation passed with synthetic/demo data (GL-206B)
Fail-closed auth baseline implemented (not production-grade IAM)
Operator token hashing and tenant binding implemented
Structured logging and rate-limit baseline implemented
Controlled external technical review allowed only with strict boundaries and synthetic/demo data
All examples use synthetic identifiers only
All tokens and keys in examples are placeholders
Security-sensitive reports route to GitHub Security Advisories
Production SaaS not claimed and not supported
Real customer/private grant/institutional data not ready
prohibited_public_claims: Production SaaS ready / production-grade / enterprise ready
Ready for real customer data / private grant data / institutional data
Claimed compliance certification, GDPR certification, SOC2 certification, or ISO certification
Official SDK available / official package published
Independent security audit completed / penetration test passed
Multi-tenant production isolation guaranteed / database RLS enforced
Public website is a production marketing launch
Any claim implying a support SLA or uptime guarantee
Any claim that implies real customer deployments are running

no_real_data_safety_assessment.assessment: No real data present in currently reviewed candidate files. Per-file real-data scan required before any export candidate is created.

no_secret_safety_assessment.assessment: No obvious secret patterns detected in reviewed candidate files. A dedicated secret-pattern scan must pass before any export candidate is created or pushed.

internal_infrastructure_leakage_assessme.assessment: Reviewed candidate files do not contain detected internal infrastructure references. Internal-path scan required before any export.

package_publish_metadata_assessment.assessment: No package/publish/deployment/cloud metadata is present in tracked candidate files or added by GL-218.

prerequisites_before_any_export: Dedicated later issue creates and scans the export candidate
Explicit approval from repository owner obtained
All forbidden-file, secret, real-data, overclaim, internal-path, and package-metadata scans pass with zero findings
Candidate preserves all Developer Preview / Controlled Preview claim boundaries

applies_to: All test fixtures and test data
All scripts run in dry-run or plan mode
Any export candidate prepared for external review

remaining_blockers.description: Static admin token in auth baseline; no OIDC/SAML/SSO/MFA
No workspace-level enforcement; no database RLS
No tenant provisioning API; no deprovision/offboarding flow
No production data retention/deletion pipeline; no DSAR
No production runbook; no SLO definition; no DR playbook
No production alerting, on-call rotation, or SLO dashboards
No independent security audit or penetration test
No GDPR, SOC2, ISO, or enterprise compliance assessment
No vetted export candidate; public snapshot requires separate gate

risk_register.description: Future export candidate may inadvertently include real secrets
risk_register.mitigation: Run gl218_public_export_safety_scan.py before any export; require explicit approval

risk_register.description: Future export may include real customer/grant/institutional data
risk_register.mitigation: Enforce synthetic/demo data only boundary; scan before export

risk_register.description: Future materials may drift to overclaim production/compliance readiness
risk_register.mitigation: Prohibited-claim list; automated scan; claim review gate required

risk_register.description: Export may include internal Forgejo URLs, private IPs, or internal paths
risk_register.mitigation: Internal-path scan required; review all new docs before export

risk_register.description: A contributor may add setup.py, pyproject.toml, or package.json
risk_register.mitigation: Scope guard tests block forbidden package files in GL-218 branch

risk_register.description: A contributor may change GitHub workflows or snapshot publish scripts
risk_register.mitigation: Scope guard tests block workflow changes in GL-218 branch

risk_register.description: An export is created before a separate gate approves it
risk_register.mitigation: GL-218 explicitly does not create an export; requires a later issue

risk_register.description: An external reviewer uses real data or deploys to shared infrastructure
risk_register.mitigation: Reviewer briefing required; strict boundary documented

risk_register.description: SDK prototype is presented as an official published package
risk_register.mitigation: Prohibited-claim list; scope guards block SDK package metadata

risk_register.description: Developer Preview materials are interpreted as compliance certification
risk_register.mitigation: Prohibited-claim list; SECURITY.md explicit non-claim language

findings.finding: GrantLayer is Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries after GL-217.
No vetted export candidate exists. A dedicated later issue and explicit owner approval are required before any export.
Reviewed candidate files preserve claim boundaries after GL-207 corrections. No prohibited claims detected.
No obvious secret patterns detected in reviewed candidate files. Secret scan required before export.
No real customer/grant/institutional data detected in reviewed candidate files.
No internal Forgejo URLs, private IPs, or private paths detected in reviewed candidate files.
No setup.py, SDK pyproject.toml, package.json, or package-lock.json in tracked files.
website-design/ directory and docs/website_design_* import/report files are present as untracked files and are excluded from GL-218.
